business.headline: Unlimited health cover? The burden of understanding still falls on customersHandcrafted ByOur Editors In the second part of 'The Unlimited Illusion' series, we examine how insurers define "unlimited" health cover, why customers often misunderstand these policies, and why the burden of decoding the fine print still falls largely on buyers.

Fuel prices up Rs 5 in 10 days, govt defends limited hike amid West Asia crisisPetrol and diesel prices were raised for the third time this month as the Centre said a 76-day freeze had cushioned consumers despite soaring crude.

Petrol, diesel, CNG prices hiked again. Check rates in your cityIndia relies on imports for nearly 85 per cent of its crude oil requirements, making domestic fuel prices vulnerable to fluctuations in global markets. The fresh hike is likely to put additional pressure on commuters, transporters and businesses across multiple sectors.

India better positioned on inflation, RBI should keep rates unchanged: ASSOCHAMASSOCHAM's analysis showed that India's retail inflation increased only slightly from 3.2% in February 2026 to 3.5% in April 2026 — a rise of just 0.3 percentage points. In comparison, inflation in the United States jumped sharply from 2.4% to 3.8% during the same period.

RBI to transfer record Rs 2.87 lakh crore dividend to govt for FY26This move is expected to give the government additional financial room at a time of geopolitical uncertainty and global economic challenges.

Don't file your ITR blindly: These 6 AIS mismatches can invite an income tax noticeThink filing your ITR is just about checking pre-filled details and clicking 'submit'? Not so fast. As the ITR filing season for FY 2026–27 begins, tax experts say ignoring even a small mismatch in your AIS could land you in trouble later.

Haryana is a home away from home for Japanese firms: CM Nayab Singh SainiSpeaking at the third India-Japan Conclave in New Delhi, organised by India Today, Saini said Haryana has become a "home away from home" for Japanese companies and stressed that the relationship between the two sides is built not only on investment, but also on trust.

8th Pay Commission: Why a full return to old pension scheme may be difficult nowAs the 8th Pay Commission widens consultations, employee unions are renewing their push for the Old Pension Scheme. But with Rs 16.5 lakh crore already inside NPS, the debate is shifting towards guaranteed pension safeguards within the existing system.
Sensex, Nifty end week higher as banking shares drive gainsThe BSE Sensex ended 231.99 points, or 0.31%, higher at 75,415.35. The Nifty 50 gained 64.60 points, or 0.27%, to close at 23,719.30.

Why your next pack of condoms may cost more amid global oil shockThe company, which sells Manforce condoms and holds around 30% market share in the country, said it may eventually pass on higher costs to customers if the situation in the Middle East worsens.

Japan bets big on India with $68 billion investment target, tech pushAt the third India-Japan Conclave organised by the India Today Group, Japan unveiled an updated Indo-Pacific strategy that places India at the heart of its push on technology, investment and regional security.
Sensex up 500 points: Why is stock market rising today?At 1:45 PM, the BSE Sensex was up 514.96 points, or 0.68%, at 75,698.32. The index touched an intraday high of 75,810.97 and a low of 75,230.75.

Is India sleepwalking into a retirement crisis?Many Indians dream of a peaceful retirement, but dreams alone do not pay medical bills or monthly expenses. So, are Indians financially ready for retirement, or just hoping for the best?

From long queues to quick clicks: How innovation is reshaping digital lendingApplying for a loan is no longer just about filling forms and waiting for approvals. From instant verification to quicker disbursals, innovation is reshaping digital lending, but what does this shift really mean for borrowers?

RBI may skip repo rate hike despite rupee fall, eye inflation: ReportThe RBI is expected to focus more on inflation and broader economic stability even as the rupee continues to hit record lows against the US dollar amid the ongoing global oil shock.

Soft landing, not free fall: Decoding RBI's rupee game planThe central bank is increasingly allowing the rupee to weaken gradually as oil prices remain high, with economists arguing that preventing panic matters more than defending a symbolic number.

8th Pay Commission consultation moves to Lucknow: Key dates, eligibility and detailsThe 8th Pay Commission is steadily expanding its consultation process, with Lucknow now added to the list of cities where employee representatives and government bodies can share their views. But who can meet the panel, and what are the key dates you should know?

Rupee at 100 against US dollar? Why experts say India shouldn't panicAs crude oil prices stay elevated and the rupee weakens further, a growing number of experts now argue India's real risk is not crossing 100 against the dollar, but rising inflation and slowing growth.

Parle Industries hits 5% upper circuit again as investors chase wrong Parle stockParle Industries shares hit the upper circuit for a third straight session after the Modi-Meloni Melody video went viral. The rally reflected confusion over the Parle name, even though Melody toffees are made by unlisted Parle Products.

Sensex, Nifty open higher as crude stays near $105; banks, financials lead gainsThe BSE Sensex was up 336.46 points at 75,519.82 around 9:26 am, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 86.30 points to trade at 23,741.00.

entertainment.headline: David Dhawan gets emotional recalling Varun during his hospital stay. Watch David Dhawan broke down in tears at the trailer launch of Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai while recalling how son Varun Dhawan stayed beside him during his 2022 hospitalisation.

Salman Khan steals the spotlight at 7 Dogs Egypt premiere as cameo goes viral Salman Khan grabbed attention at the Egypt premiere of 7 Dogs despite skipping the event, as giant posters featuring the actor dominated the venue.

Papa was special: Bobby Deol breaks down remembering Dharmendra Bobby Deol became emotional while remembering father Dharmendra during a conversation with Shekhar Suman on the show Shekhar Tonite. He said people still speak of his father with deep emotion.

Ram Charan's Peddi gets U/A 16+ certificate after minor censor cuts, runtime locked Peddi, starring Ram Charan and Janhvi Kapoor, has received a U/A 16+ certificate ahead of its theatrical release. The makers also revealed the film's final runtime after CBFC-recommended cuts and modifications.

Nepali film Elephants in the Fog makes history, wins Jury Prize at Cannes Elephants in the Fog won the Un Certain Regard Jury Prize at the Cannes Film Festival, becoming the first Nepali film to receive an award at the festival.

Vir Das on Cockroach Janta Party crackdown: Dumb move to shut down their accounts Vir Das called the alleged crackdown on Cockroach Janata Party social media accounts a "dumb move," saying it only strengthened the movement, after founder Abhijeet Dipke claimed several CJP accounts were hacked or withheld.

Aishwarya Rai and Aaradhya radiate elegance in new pics from Cannes Aishwarya Rai Bachchan attended L'Oréal Paris' Lights on Women's Worth awards with Aaradhya Bachchan in coordinated cape-style looks. Pictures of the two from the event have surfaced online.

Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai trailer: Varun's film gives major Garam Masala vibes The trailer of Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai is out. Directed by David Dhawan, the film sets up a comedy of hidden relationships, pregnancy twists and 1990s musical recall ahead of its June 5 release.

Rukmini Vasanth calls out viral deepfake bikini pics, threatens legal action In a statement on social media, Rukmini Vasanth called out the viral bikini pictures claiming to be her. The actor called it fake and fabricated.

The Boroughs review: Stranger Things but trade teen chaos for a tale of ageing, lossRelease Date:Thu May 21, 2026 Netflix's The Boroughs follows a grieving widower and his fellow retirees as they investigate a terrifying creature haunting their community. Though uneven and slow at times, the eight-episode sci-fi show offers a thoughtful take on ageing, grief and loneliness.

Karuppu box office Day 8: Suriya film enters 2nd week with momentum Suriya's Karuppu has managed to maintain its spot at the worldwide box office on Day 8. The steady second-week hold and strong word of mouth have strengthened its comeback narrative.

Chand Mera Dil box office Day 1: Ananya-Lakshya film outpaces Ek Din opening Ananya Panday and Lakshya Lalwani's Chand Mera Dil opened in theatres with Rs 2.90 crore nett in India on day 1. The modest start put it ahead of Junaid Khan and Sai Pallavi's Ek Din despite mixed reviews.

Pan-India before pan-India: Malayalam cinema comes full circle with Drishyam 3 Mohanlal and Jeethu Joseph's Drishyam 3 brings renewed focus to Malayalam cinema's long national reach. Its arrival underlines how rooted, emotionally precise stories travelled across India long before pan-India became a formula.

Aishwarya Rai, Eva Longoria turn Cannes 2026 into best friend reunion Eva Longoria and Aishwarya Rai Bachchan shared a warm exchange during a Cannes photo opportunity. A video from the beautiful moment showed Eva referring to Aishwarya as her "best friend" at the festival.

Drishyam 3 box office Day 2: Mohanlal's film wins overseas, Rs 75 crore worldwide Mohanlal's Drishyam 3 is rewriting box office records overseas, earning a staggering Rs 75.94 crore worldwide in just two days. With Georgekutty pulling crowds globally, the thriller is now racing towards the Rs 100-crore mark.

Still soaking in love: Karishma Tanna gives insight into her baby shower ceremony Actor Karishma Tanna shared a video from her South Indian-style baby shower ceremony. She and husband Varun Bangera are gearing up to welcome their first child.

Aishwarya Rai's Cannes 2026 look drips in blue drama and main-character energy Aishwarya Rai Bachchan, India's Cannes veteran, made her first appearance at the ongoing film festival in a structured blue gown. A video of her look went viral on social media on Friday night.

Madhuri Dixit, Triptii Dimri trolled as co-star Dharna gets snubbed by paps At a Mumbai promotion for Maa Behen, paparazzi asked for a frame with only Madhuri Dixit and Triptii Dimri. Several social media users have now questioned the silence around the exclusion of influencer-actor Dharna Durga from the photo-call.

Fact check: Truth behind Rukmini Vasanth's viral bikini pics Visuals claiming to show Rukmini Vasanth during a poolside photoshoot have surfaced online. However, the viral clip appears to be AI-generated and is not connected to the actor in any verified manner.

general news.headline: PM gets White House invite as Rubio pitches for US energy amid Iran war Live UpdatesThe three-day visit to India by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio comes at a time when ties between India and the US under Trump 2.0 have continued to fluctuate amid trade tensions.

Power games at Delhi Gymkhana over. Delhi Gymkhana itself may be historyMore than a year after the NCLAT deadline for reforms and fresh elections passed, the battle over Delhi Gymkhana Club has culminated in the Centre ordering the elite institution to vacate its Lutyens' Delhi premises by June 5. Here's how years of bureaucratic takeover, litigation and failed reform attempts brought one of India's most exclusive clubs to this point.
Laugh with Cockroach Janta Party, then move on to the next jokeCockroach Janta Party has taken over the Indian social media space. It is a joke, you should laugh with it and then move on.

97% votes to BJP: The Muslim-majority booth that flipped a Bengal seatThe Rajarhat New Town seat in Bengal swung to the BJP after booth 164 in Musalman Para was counted in the final round on May 5. The result has triggered questions from the TMC and fresh scrutiny of the counting process.

I went home to saffron Bengal and this is what I sawBJP pulled off the unthinkable and took Bengal. I went home days after May 4 to a saffron Kolkata. The poriborton I saw was subtle but not one to be dismissed.

US energy products can diversify India's energy needs: Marco Rubio's pitch to PMThe US Secretary of State advocated for American energy exports to help diversify India's energy basket during his meeting with PM Modi, reiterating his earlier assertion that Washington wants to sell India "as much energy as they'll buy" amid mounting uncertainty over disruptions in the Hormuz.

10-year-old girl abducted 2 days ago found dead in water tank in Tamil NaduA 10-year-old girl was abducted by her neighbour while playing outside her house in Coimbatore, before being sexually assaulted and murdered, police said.

Vinesh Phogat vs WFI: Delhi HC green lights wrestler for Asiad trialsThe Delhi High Court has slammed the Wrestling Federation of India for their vindictive attitude against wrestler Vinesh Phogat. The court has allowed Phogat to compete in the Asian Games trials despite the WFI deeming her ineligible for the competitions.

Mamata Banerjee-designed football sculpture demolished near Kolkata stadiumThe BJP government on Saturday removed a controversial football-themed sculpture, designed by Mamata Banerjee, from the iconic Salt Lake stadium. It was installed ahead of the 2017 FIFA U-17 World Cup.

'Honorary cockroach' Sonam Wangchuk has a message for govtAs the viral "cockroach" movement gathers momentum online, Sonam Wangchuk has stepped into the debate with a sharp warning for the government and an unusual declaration that is now fuelling fresh attention.

Unlimited health cover? The burden of understanding still falls on customersHandcrafted ByOur Editors In the second part of 'The Unlimited Illusion' series, we examine how insurers define "unlimited" health cover, why customers often misunderstand these policies, and why the burden of decoding the fine print still falls largely on buyers.

For defence infra: Govt orders British-era Delhi Gymkhana Club to vacate premisesOne of India's most exclusive colonial-era clubs is staring at a major shake-up after the Centre ordered it to vacate its Lutyens' Delhi premises, citing defence infrastructure and urgent governance needs.

More than a view: How Dharamsala's stadium rewrote the story of a Himalayan townHandcrafted ByOur Editors The HPCA Stadium in Dharamsala is routinely called the world's most beautiful cricket ground. But its real achievement has nothing to do with the view, and everything to do with what it built around it.

Pan-India before pan-India: Malayalam cinema comes full circle with Drishyam 3Mohanlal and Jeethu Joseph's Drishyam 3 brings renewed focus to Malayalam cinema's long national reach. Its arrival underlines how rooted, emotionally precise stories travelled across India long before pan-India became a formula.

Cockroach Janta Party says website taken down, founder slams 'dictatorial' govtThe Cockroach Janta Party said its website and social media accounts were taken down on Saturday. The claim drew fresh online backing for the student-led satire movement born from anger over exams and unemployment.

Why Himanta govt named a Guwahati flyover after a Bengali nationalist leaderAssam's Chief Minister, Himanta Biswa Sarma, has named Guwahati's new Rs 376 crore flyover after Syama Prasad Mookerjee, the founder of the Bharatiya Jana Sangh (BJS). It's not just because the BJS was the BJP's precursor. There's a reason in the history you might want to know.

IIT, IAS, MBA: The hidden dowry rate card still haunting Indian marriagesHandcrafted ByOur Editors Dowry may be illegal in India, but unofficial "rates" linked to education and jobs still survive. From IAS officers to IIT graduates, groom value often rises with degrees and salaries. This article explores dowry deaths, academic research, and why families are questioning a system that still monetises educated men.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Bandra Talao drained for first-ever rejuvenation as pollution wipes out aquatic lifeMumbai's Bandra Talao is being dewatered and desilted after a survey found no surviving aquatic life. The restoration aims to revive the lake's ecosystem and address years of pollution and stagnation.

Exclusive: First photo of rooftop where Twisha Sharma was found deadThe first visuals of the rooftop at Twisha Sharma's marital home have surfaced as the probe intensifies. A court-ordered second autopsy and her husband's arrest have sharpened focus on the contested death case.

A cape, satin couture, crystal florals define Aishwarya Rai Bachchan's second Cannes outingAishwarya Rai Bachchan attended Lights on Women's Worth at Cannes in a blush pink Sophie Couture gown with Aaradhya Bachchan by her side. The appearance marked a shift towards a more sculpted festival silhouette in her 24th year at the event.

Disrespectful, come back: Sridhar Vembu to H1-B Indians in USZoho founder Sridhar Vembu has responded to the Trump administration's new rule for the Green Card. Vembu has urged Indians to let "self-respect" dictate their decision and return to India.

Don't play with fire: Humayun Kabir warns Bengal govt on Eid prayer, qurbaniThe confrontation between Humayun Kabir and the BJP government in West Bengal snowballed as the Opposition MLA warned it over cow slaughter restrictions, curbs on Eid namaz and mandatory singing of Vande Mataram in madrasas.

Hizbul chief, Al-Badr supremo: Top terrorists attend Pulwama mastermind's funeralSeveral wanted Pakistan-based terrorists attended Pulwama attack mastermind Hamza Burhan's funeral prayers in Islamabad after he was shot in Pakistan-occupied Kashmir. The gathering's heavy security underscored rising fears amid a string of targeted killings of terrorists in the neighbouring country.

sports.headline: Jofra Archer not bothered by sharp criticism amid IPL vs England controversy IPL 2026, MI vs RR: Rajasthan Royals assistant coach Trevor Penney said Jofra Archer is unfazed by criticism in the ongoing IPL vs England debate, insisting he remains focused and unaffected by outside noise.

Vinesh Phogat vs WFI: Delhi HC green lights wrestler for Asiad trials The Delhi High Court has slammed the Wrestling Federation of India for their vindictive attitude against wrestler Vinesh Phogat. The court has allowed Phogat to compete in the Asian Games trials despite the WFI deeming her ineligible for the competitions.

Why Jasprit Bumrah has struggled in IPL 2026? Mumbai Indians head coach explains IPL 2026, MI vs RR: Mumbai Indians head coach Mahela Jayawardene said Jasprit Bumrah's struggles were due to a combination of a post–T20 World Cup niggle and the time needed to regain rhythm after injury.

Bruno Fernandes named Premier League player of the season after historic turnaround Manchester United star Bruno Fernandes has been named the Premier League Player of the Season. After a tough start under Ruben Amorim, the midfielder was moved back to his best position by Michael Carrick, matching a historic 20-assist record.

FIFA World Cup: Congo asked to undergo 21-day isolation due to Ebola outbreak Congo's World Cup 2026 preparations have been disrupted after US authorities ordered a 21-day isolation bubble for the squad in Belgium amid an Ebola outbreak in the African nation.

Indian fans may finally have clarity on FIFA World Cup 2026 broadcast rights Indian football fans may finally get clarity on FIFA World Cup 2026 broadcast rights, with former AIFF general secretary, Shaji Prabhakaran claiming a broadcaster announcement is expected in India next week.

Virat Kohli brings Australian-style fire in fiery Travis Head exchange IPL 2026, SRH vs SRH Irfan Pathan defended Virat Kohli's fiery on-field exchanges with Travis Head during RCB's clash against the Sunrisers, saying such banter is natural in high-pressure matches and reflects Kohli's aggressive Australian-style intensity.

More than a view: How Dharamsala's stadium rewrote the story of a Himalayan town The HPCA Stadium in Dharamsala is routinely called the world's most beautiful cricket ground. But its real achievement has nothing to do with the view, and everything to do with what it built around it.

Venkatesh Iyer sets an example of professionalism amidst IPL's glamour and spotlight SRH vs RCB: Andy Flower praised Venkatesh Iyer for his professionalism, patience, and impact after the batter's quickfire 44 helped Royal Challengers Bengaluru secure a top-two finish and Qualifier 1 spot in IPL 2026.

When MS Dhoni is there why search more? Ashwin wants Thala as new CSK head coach Ravichandran Ashwin believes CSK's search for a new head coach will be simple if they move on from Stephen Fleming. The spinner cheekily posted on X that with MS Dhoni already in the dugout, the franchise has no reason to look anywhere else.

Are we slaves? Iga Swiatek blasts Dubai tournament director after penalties threatFrench Open 2026: Iga Swiatek slammed Dubai Championships director Salah Tahlak for suggesting stricter penalties on players withdrawing from tournaments, defending their right to prioritise fitness and manage schedules.

Carrick's at the wheel: Another Solksjaer reign or a new era for United?Michael Carrick is finally going to be given a fair crack at Manchester United as he has been made the new full-time head coach. Can Carrick continue his fine start and prove that it isn't another false dawn at the club?

Punjab Kings battle chaos and pressure with playoff hopes on the line vs LSGLSG vs PBKS, Preview: Punjab Kings head into a must-win clash against Lucknow Super Giants on Saturday, hoping to stop a six-match losing streak that has pushed their playoff hopes to the brink. While PBKS still have an outside chance of qualifying, LSG, already out of contention, will look to end their season on a high note at home.

IPL playoff scenario: How Punjab Kings can secure 4th spotIPL 2026, LSG vs PBKS: Shreyas Iyer's Punjab Kings will have everything to play for when they take on Lucknow Super Giants on Saturday, May 23, with a playoff spot at stake.

RCB won the bigger battle ahead of IPL playoffs despite loss to SRH: SehwagVirender Sehwag says RCB won the bigger battle despite their loss to SRH, with a 200-run chase securing top spot. He also backed Virat Kohli's pragmatic approach ahead of the IPL playoffs.

Watch: Kavya Maran's priceless reaction to Krunal Pandya's bouncerSRH vs RCB: Sunrisers Hyderabad CEO, Kavya Maran was taken by surprise after seeing Krunal Pandya bowl his bouncer at the Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium. Her reaction has gone viral on social media.

IPL Play Of The Day: Heinrich Klaasen makes Hazlegod look human in Uppal carnageSRH vs RCB, IPL Play of the Day: Heinrich Klaasen proved that Josh Hazlewood was indeed human as he destroyed the Australian pacer during the SRH vs RCB clash. Klaasen's takedown of Hazlewood proved to be the difference as Hyderabad enter Eliminator in destructive form.

Virat Kohli ignores handshake with Travis Head, moment goes viral: WatchVirat Kohli sparked controversy as he ignored the customary post-match handshake with Travis Head following the SRH vs RCB game in Hyderabad.

RCB's 255 chase approach against SRH split fans over intentIPL 2026: RCB secured the bigger prize of Qualifier 1 despite a crushing 55-run defeat to Sunrisers Hyderabad. But their surprisingly cautious approach in a 256-run chase has now sparked a major debate among fans online.

Sabalenka cuts short media duties in protest against French Open prize moneyAryna Sabalenka cut short her French Open media duties as players staged a 15-minute work-to-rule protest over prize money. The action underlined a wider push for a bigger share of Grand Slam revenue, benefits and influence.

Dream wicket for me: Sakib Hussain relishing Virat Kohli's dismissalIPL 2026: Sakib Hussain dismissed Virat Kohli in Sunrisers Hyderabad's 55-run win over Royal Challengers Bengaluru. The wicket fulfilled a personal dream for the SRH fast-bowler even as the franchise failed to secure a top-two finish.

RCB get Qualifier 1, but SRH arrive at playoffs like absolute villainsIPL 2026: RCB may have secured the safer Qualifier 1 route, but it was Sunrisers Hyderabad who walked away looking like the playoffs' biggest danger. Abhishek Sharma, Ishan Kishan and Heinrich Klaasen tore apart the defending champions in a brutal Hyderabad batting show.

Virat Kohli and Travis Head share verbal banter during SRH vs RCBVirat Kohli repeatedly needled Travis Head during RCB's chase at Uppal, even gesturing for the Australian to bowl off-spin. The exchanges sharpened the mood of a high-stakes night shaped by SRH's 256 and Kohli's early dismissal.

Chinnaswamy revamp begins days after Bengaluru loses IPL 2026 finalIPL 2026: Days after Bengaluru lost hosting rights for the IPL 2026 final, the iconic M Chinnaswamy Stadium is now heading towards a major makeover. KSCA president Venkatesh Prasad admitted that the venue's limited capacity may have contributed to the BCCI shifting the summit clash to Ahmedabad.

OG T20 league IPL reason behind India's consecutive World Cup wins: GuptillMartin Guptill said the IPL has directly shaped India's back-to-back T20 World Cup wins. He argued the league's scale, talent pipeline and competitive edge keep it ahead of every rival T20 league in the world.

ISL clubs warn AIFF that uncertainty could impact long-term league commitmentSeveral ISL clubs have warned the AIFF they may scale back their commitment beyond the current season. The statement sharpens pressure ahead of the Special General Body Meeting on the league's commercial future.

Indian football board set for crucial meeting on ISL commercial rights battleThe AIFF will hold a Special General Body Meeting on Saturday to discuss the ISL's commercial future and proposed constitutional changes. The meeting comes amid opposition from clubs over rights structure and objections over the General Body's composition.

Animesh Kujur breaks national 100m record to seal Commonwealth games qualificationAnimesh Kujur reclaimed the 100m national record minutes after Gurindervir Singh took it at the Federation Cup. His 10.15 seconds sealed Commonwealth Games qualification and sharpened India's emerging sprint rivalry.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi sparks Bihar IPL dream as CM makes big claimBihar Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary on Friday backed the idea of having an Indian Premier League (IPL) team from the state, saying the government is working in "mission mode" to improve cricket infrastructure and create opportunities for young players.

KKR suffer big blow as Angkrish Raghuvanshi ruled out of must-win DC clashKolkata Knight Riders have lost Angkrish Raghuvanshi to a concussion and a fractured finger ahead of the Delhi Capitals game. The setback weakens both their batting and wicketkeeping options with playoff qualification still on the line.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
